Now, let's think about the DSU implementation. We'll need a parent array and a rank array. The find function will perform path compression, and the union function will merge smaller rank into larger.

In Java, the code would be:

Implement the DSU as a helper class or with arrays.

Now, let's outline the code.

The function is:

public boolean[] friendRequests(int n, int[][] restrictions, int[][] requests) {

   // Initialize DSU.

   int[] parent = new int[n];
   int[] rank = new int[n];
   for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
       parent[i] = i;
       rank[i] = 1;
   }

   boolean[] result = new boolean[requests.length];

   for (int j = 0; j < requests.length; j++) {
       int u = requests[j][0];
       int v = requests[j][1];

       if (find(parent, u) == find(parent, v)) {
           result[j] = true;
           continue;
       }

       boolean canConnect = true;

       for (int[] restriction : restrictions) {
           int x = restriction[0];
           int y = restriction[1];

           int rootX = find(parent, x);
           int rootY = find(parent, y);

           int rootU = find(parent, u);
           int rootV = find(parent, v);

           if ( (rootX == rootU && rootY == rootV) || (rootX == rootV && rootY == rootU) ) {
               canConnect = false;
               break;
           }
       }

       if (canConnect) {
           union(parent, rank, u, v);
           result[j] = true;
       } else {
           result[j] = false;
       }
   }

   return result;
}

class Solution {
    public boolean[] friendRequests(int n, int[][] restrictions, int[][] requests) {
        int[] parent = new int[n];
        int[] rank = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            parent[i] = i;
            rank[i] = 1;
        }
        
        boolean[] result = new boolean[requests.length];
        
        for (int j = 0; j < requests.length; j++) {
            int u = requests[j][0];
            int v = requests[j][1];
            
            if (find(parent, u) == find(parent, v)) {
                result[j] = true;
                continue;
            }
            
            boolean canConnect = true;
            
            for (int[] restriction : restrictions) {
                int x = restriction[0];
                int y = restriction[1];
                
                int rootX = find(parent, x);
                int rootY = find(parent, y);
                int rootU = find(parent, u);
                int rootV = find(parent, v);
                
                if ((rootX == rootU && rootY == rootV) || (rootX == rootV && rootY == rootU)) {
                    canConnect = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
            
            if (canConnect) {
                union(parent, rank, u, v);
                result[j] = true;
            } else {
                result[j] = false;
            }
        }
        
        return result;
    }
    
    private int find(int[] parent, int x) {
        if (parent[x] != x) {
            parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x]);
        }
        return parent[x];
    }
    
    private void union(int[] parent, int[] rank, int x, int y) {
        int rootX = find(parent, x);
        int rootY = find(parent, y);
        
        if (rootX == rootY) {
            return;
        }
        
        if (rank[rootX] > rank[rootY]) {
            parent[rootY] = rootX;
        } else if (rank[rootX] < rank[rootY]) {
            parent[rootX] = rootY;
        } else {
            parent
